check.cmd: python3 -c "import sys,json,glob; sys.path.insert(0,'plugin/src'); from lume.validate import validate_entity, load_schema; s=load_schema('gap'); assert 'workstreams' in s['properties'] and 'resolution' in s['properties'] and 'workstreams' not in s.get('required',[]); [validate_entity('gap', json.load(open(f))) for f in glob.glob('.lume/gaps/*.json')]; print('ok')"

items.text: schemas/gap.json gains optional 'workstreams' (array of workstream-id strings) and optional structured 'resolution' object (kind: implemented|wont-fix|superseded|duplicate; note; optional workstream) within the validator subset; all existing .lume/gaps/ records still validate unchanged
'lume gap link <source> <id> -w <ws>' appends the resolved workstream id to the gap's workstreams list (idempotent; unknown workstream or gap is a named error, not a traceback)

check.cmd: python3 -m pytest -q tests/ -k 'gap' 2>/dev/null | tail -1 | grep -q passed

items.text: 'lume gap resolve <source> <id> [-w <ws>] ["note"]' persists a structured resolution (kind implemented when -w given, note stored - the silently-dropped-text bug is fixed); plain resolve without args still works

'lume status -w <ws>' detail lists the gaps whose workstreams include it (derived by scanning .lume/gaps/, read-only, same pattern as the children rollup); workstreams answering no gap render unchanged

check.cmd: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('.lume/gaps/lume-G9.json')); assert '0018' in d.get('workstreams',[]), 'not linked'; r=d.get('resolution'); assert isinstance(r,dict) and r.get('kind')=='implemented', r; print('ok')"

items.text: Dogfood: G9 is linked to this workstream (0018) via lume gap link and resolved via the new structured resolve, so its record carries workstreams:['0018'] and a resolution object with kind 'implemented'

check.cmd: grep -q 'gap link' plugin/skills/lume/SKILL.md && grep -q 'gap link' plugin/README.md && python3 -m pytest -q

items.text: Docs describe the linkage (gap link verb, structured resolve, the demand-backlog framing) in plugin/skills/lume/SKILL.md and plugin/README.md; tests cover schema back-compat, link, structured resolve, status surface, and error paths; full suite green
The pre-existing uncommitted loop residue (0017 accept/close state flips, gap G9) is committed on this iteration; nothing under .lume/gaps or .lume/reviews left uncommitted at handback

dod.preamble: Answer G9: gap-to-workstream linkage as data. Optional schema fields (back-compatible), gap link verb, structured resolve (fixing the dropped-resolution-text bug), derived status surface, dogfooded on G9 itself.

handback: Gap-to-workstream linkage shipped and dogfooded: schema fields (workstreams + structured resolution), lume gap link, structured lume gap resolve (note no longer dropped), and a 'Gaps answered' section in workstream status. G9 resolved through the new mechanism itself - its record carries workstreams:['0018'] and a structured resolution. 416 tests green; docs updated. One deliberate break disclosed in the self-review: string resolutions are no longer valid (G1/G8 + one test fixture migrated; no other repo affected).
self_review: All seven DoD checks pass machine-verified (0 failed / 7); suite 416 passed (was 401; 16 added, one fixture updated). Implementation follows the established idioms: optional schema fields (no migration needed for records without them), single-direction refs on the gap with the workstream side derived by scan (mirrors parent/children), and the structured resolve subsumes the dropped-note bug. Honest disclosures: (1) the DoD said existing gap records 'validate unchanged' - all do EXCEPT G1/G8, whose legacy STRING resolutions (set by hand last iteration) were migrated to the structured object shape, and one adopter-fixture in test_gap_scan.py was updated the same way; string resolutions are no longer schema-valid, which is the deliberate trade (one shape, no oneOf in the validator subset) but is a breaking change for any adopter repo carrying a string resolution - none exist beyond the two migrated here (tredl has no gap records). (2) 'gap resolve' reuses the global -t flag for the resolution kind; -t means iteration type elsewhere - consistent with flag reuse precedent but worth knowing. (3) The queue plan from review ingest still emits plain 'gap add' commands; auto-capture of review_gaps (G6) and ingest linking are not in scope here.
